subject: English
notes: Core section | 50 questions | 35 minutes | Score range: 1–36

sub_topics: Topic Development — determining the purpose and objective of a text passage
Assessing whether a passage or section has achieved its intended purpose
Evaluating relevance of material to the text's main theme
Rhetorical devices and their effective use in writing
Organisation, Unity, and Cohesion — ensuring logical flow of a text
Selecting effective introductory, transitional, and concluding sentences
Maintaining unity within and between paragraphs
Argumentative essay structure (new in 2025 Enhanced ACT)
Identifying and improving weak or off-topic supporting details
Selecting precise and concise vocabulary in context
Maintaining consistent tone and style throughout a passage
Avoiding redundancy and wordiness
Choosing words that fit the purpose and intended audience
Matching language register to formal or informal contexts
Avoiding ambiguous pronoun references
Sentence Structure and Formation — correcting run-ons, fragments, and fused sentences
Parallel structure in lists and paired constructions
Subordination and coordination of clauses
Modifier placement — avoiding dangling and misplaced modifiers
Punctuation — correct use of commas, semicolons, colons, apostrophes, and dashes
End punctuation usage
Usage — subject-verb agreement
Pronoun-antecedent agreement and pronoun case
Verb tense consistency
Adjective vs. adverb usage
Commonly confused words (e.g., affect/effect, its/it's, who/whom)
